checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-011 define observable routing order, tier mappings and floors, escalation transitions, identity/isolation behavior, manifest validation, report metrics, validator parity, and release surfaces; AC-001 through AC-007 and TEST-001 through TEST-006 provide concrete validation targets for those stated requirements.

The Goal requires every independent review to start from a "bounded, file-backed context," but AC-002 and TEST-002 cover only fresh/read-only reviewer contexts, unique run/session identities, and rejection of same-session fallback; they do not require evidence that reviewer inputs are file-backed and bounded by an allowed-input manifest or equivalent artifact. A verifier therefore cannot decide what acceptance evidence proves this explicit goal clause. This is a Phase 1 goal-to-acceptance coverage gap, not a later implementation detail, and is Major because part of the stated feature outcome can pass all listed criteria without being demonstrated.

AC-001 through AC-007 specify inspectable structural-test results, identifier uniqueness, fail-closed diagnostics, manifest acceptance/rejection, required template fields, repository/version validation, and rollback behavior rather than subjective intent.

The Non-goals explicitly exclude globally pinned model IDs, reviewer/evaluator implementation fallback, and chat-log inputs; REQ-005 and REQ-006 also bound capable-host versus unsupported-host behavior, while REQ-011 and AC-007 bound release and rollback scope.

The Goal imposes bounded file-backed context on every independent review and REQ-005 says reviewer/evaluator isolation never permits fallback, but REQ-007 defines allowed paths, hashes, snapshots, and fail-closed validation only for "Each task input manifest." No requirement states the corresponding reviewer/evaluator input boundary, required file-backed artifact, allowed-path/hash rules, or rejection behavior. A designer and task author cannot determine the mandatory review-context contract. This missing isolation constraint belongs in the Phase 1 specification and is Major because implementations can satisfy the task-manifest rules while interpreting the independent-review boundary incompatibly.

The Goal's independent-review context isolation is a high-risk integrity claim, yet AC-002/TEST-002 validate freshness, read-only roles, identities, and no same-session fallback without validating that reviewer inputs are bounded and file-backed. Consequently a verifier has no planned inspection or acceptance surface for that claim. This is a specification-stage validation omission and is Major because unbounded or chat-derived reviewer context could pass the stated tests while violating the feature's isolation objective.
